verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase predominantly resident in trans-Golgi, but the long-form isoform (with an extended cytoplasmic tail) is well-documented to traffic to the plasma membrane with its catalytic ectodomain facing the extracellular space, where it functions as a surface recognition receptor for oligosaccharide ligands on opposing cells and ECM (sperm-egg recognition, cell migration, MCF-7 surface receptor activity). UniProt explicitly annotates 'Cell membrane; Single-pass type II membrane protein' and 'Cell surface'. Dual Golgi/PM localization with its own TM anchor at the surface qualifies as contextual/dual_localization.
